subject: Project Management
title: Sprint Retrospective
description: A quick win: understand Sprint Retrospective.

hook.text: After the review, the team turns the mirror on itself: how did we work together?

buildup.text: What went well? What went badly? What one thing will we improve next sprint?

discovery.text: The retro is Scrum's built-in learning loop — kaizen baked into the framework.

twist.text: Teams that skip retros stop improving. Teams that hold them compound small gains into big leaps.

climax.text: The retrospective is where good teams become great — one sprint at a time.

punchline.text: Improvement isn't a project. It's a habit.

quiz.question: What makes the Sprint Retrospective unique among Scrum events?
options: It focuses on team process improvement, not the product
